title: Beautiful Aerial View of The Horseshoe Bend! #shorts
creator: THE NATURE PROJECT
notes: MANUALLY CURATED WORKED EXAMPLE -- one exemplar only, per spec AC3.4/Scope-out (no automated scraping/bulk downloading was performed to produce this record). Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusted from the prior-agent candidate JSON) via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/Bsw-Nh2zve8&format=json), which returned title="Beautiful Aerial View of The Horseshoe Bend! #shorts" and author_name="THE NATURE PROJECT" (author_url https://www.youtube.com/@THENATUREPROJECTFILMS) -- an exact match to both fields in the candidate record, confirming this source_url this session rather than trusting the prior pass verbatim. This is a native YouTube Shorts upload (vertical, sub-60s runtime as implied by the Shorts URL path and oEmbed player aspect ratio of 113x200) showing a single, fast-cut aerial shot of the Horseshoe Bend meander in the Colorado River desert canyon (Arizona), fitting the desert/canyon/mesa landscapes subgenre. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': this session fetched the raw watch-page HTML for this URL directly (curl with a desktop User-Agent) and grepped the full ~1.39MB response case-insensitively for 'creative commons', finding zero matches -- i.e. no explicit CC-license marking was found on this specific upload, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. A secondary attempt this session to fetch the channel page (https://www.youtube.com/@THENATUREPROJECTFILMS) to extract a subscriber count as a weak polish/reach signal returned a ~1.25MB response with no subscriberCountText/subscriberCount field extractable via grep (the channel page likely requires JS rendering to populate that data) -- this attempt is disclosed rather than omitted, and no subscriber-count claim is made anywhere in this record as a result. `duration` is left null: YouTube oEmbed does not return a duration field for this URL and no YouTube Data API call was made this session -- rather than infer a number from the Shorts sub-60s convention, this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md refresh/expiry process). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is ordinary recurring channel content (Shorts-format nature/travel drone clips), not a documented award, festival selection, or press-featured showcase -- inventing a provenance string here would violate the no-placeholders constitution rule. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments, calibrated toward the lower end of this pack's existing 0.72-0.88 range (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.76): unlike the pack's BBC Earth/SkyPixel-contest/multi-award-winning independent-cinematographer exemplars, this record carries no documented award, no stated drone model or output resolution, and no verifiable reach signal (the subscriber-count check above came back empty) -- only a plausible, unverified impression of competent recurring-channel production quality from the thumbnail/title/genre alone, so it is scored toward this pack's low end rather than assumed comparable to its contest-winning/broadcaster exemplars. These are NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.76, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python` in this session. NOTE -- this diverges from what the pack's existing 10 records' notes describe: `default_weights()` called with no arguments in this session returned weights_version='default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each), not 'default-v1' (1/3 each, composition=0.0) as those older records' notes state -- i.e. this repo's Milestone-2 composition-scoring weight profile is now the module default, evidently landed after those 10 records were authored. This does not change the arithmetic outcome here: `compute_composite_score` skips any signal whose score is None regardless of its weight and renormalizes by the weight actually applied, so with composition=None the three non-composition signals remain in the same relative 1:1:1 proportion under v2 (0.25/0.25/0.25 of a 0.75 applied total) as under v1 (1/3 each of a 1.0 applied total) -- both yield the plain arithmetic mean of sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness. The call returned composite_score=0.7399999999999999 (rounded to 0.74 here), matching (0.74+0.72+0.76)/3 exactly -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements, and even though the underlying weights profile version has moved on from what this pack's earlier notes assumed.
